framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Build open-source scientific-computing tools the field runs on
paths.what_it_is: Build and maintain interactive-computing or data-tooling infrastructure — notebooks, kernels, parallel-computing libraries — that other scientists rely on daily, growing it from a side project into real infrastructure.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn a personal tool into infrastructure an entire field depends on — reach that goes far beyond your own research results.

paths.path: Apply physics training to quantitative finance
paths.what_it_is: Apply the modeling and statistical-physics toolkit from your physics background to build trading systems, risk models, or pricing tools in finance.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn abstract physics modeling into tools that move real money under real constraints — a concrete, high-stakes test of your quantitative skill.

paths.path: Communicate physics to a broad public audience
paths.what_it_is: Build a durable public platform — blog, podcast, or book — that explains physics to a general audience, alongside your research or industry work.
paths.why_it_signals: Making physics legible to non-specialists proves you understand it well enough to compress it, extending your value beyond people who already read papers in your subfield.
